ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Respondent
Notes: NA=Not Applicable

ElementDescription: How often did you reassure the patient?
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = 1-3 times a month; 2 = 1-2 times a week; 3 = 3-6 times a week; 4 = Daily

ElementDescription: How often did you provide items for patient's compulsions?
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = 1-3 times a month; 2 = 1-2 times a week; 3 = 3-6 times a week; 4 = Daily

ElementDescription: How often did you participate in behaviors related to patient's compulsions?
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = 1-3 times a month; 2 = 1-2 times a week; 3 = 3-6 times a week; 4 = Daily

ElementDescription: How often did you assist the patient in avoiding things that might make him/her more anxious?
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = 1-3 times a month; 2 = 1-2 times a week; 3 = 3-6 times a week; 4 = Daily

ElementDescription: Have you avoided doing things, going places, or being with people because of the patient's OCD?
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = 1-3 times a month; 2 = 1-2 times a week; 3 = 3-6 times a week; 4 = Daily

ElementDescription: Have you modified your family routine because of the patient's symptoms?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Have you had to do some things for the family that are usually the patient's responsibility?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Have you modified your work schedule because of the patient's needs?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Have you modified your leisure activities because of the patient's needs?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Does helping the patient in these ways cause you distress?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Has your child become distressed when you have not provided assistance? To what degree?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Has the patient become angry/abusive when you have not provided assistance? To what degree?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Has the patient spent more time completing rituals when you have not provided assistance? How much more?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Mild; 2 = Moderate; 3 = Severe; 4 = Extreme

ElementDescription: Family Accommodation Scale - OCD Total Score
Notes: Sum of items fas_ocd_1-9 and 11-13 (item 10 is not included in the total score)

ElementDescription: Providing reassurance that obsessions are unfounded
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Watching the patient complete rituals
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Waiting for the patient
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Refraining from saying or doing things
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Participating in patient compulsions
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Helping patient to undertake compulsions
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Facilitating avoidance
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Tolerating patient's aberrant behaviors
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Helping patient with simple tasks/decisions
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Assuming patient's responsibilities
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Modifying personal routine because of patient symptoms
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: Modifying family routine because of patient symptoms
Notes: 0 = None; 1 = Once/week; 2 = 2-3 times/week; 3 = 4-6 times/week; 4 = Every day

ElementDescription: FAS-R OCD Total Score
Notes: Sum of items fasr_ocd_1-12

ElementDescription: Visit name
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily
